local_names.te: జాతీయ అంధత్వ దృష్టి లోప నియంత్రణ కార్యక్రమం
local_names.mr: राष्ट्रीय अंधत्व व दृष्टिदोष नियंत्रण कार्यक्रम

local_names.kn: ರಾಷ್ಟ್ರೀಯ ಅಂಧತ್ವ ಮತ್ತು ದೃಷ್ಟಿ ದೋಷ ನಿಯಂತ್ರಣ ಕಾರ್ಯಕ್ರಮ
local_names.ml: ദേശീയ അന്ധത നിയന്ത്രണ കാഴ്ച വൈകല്യ പരിപാടി
local_names.pa: ਰਾਸ਼ਟਰੀ ਅੰਨ੍ਹੇਪਣ ਅਤੇ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਨੁਕਸ ਕੰਟਰੋਲ ਪ੍ਰੋਗਰਾਮ
local_names.or: ଜାତୀୟ ଅନ୍ଧତ୍ୱ ଓ ଦୃଷ୍ଟି ବିକଳାଙ୍ଗ ନିୟନ୍ତ୍ରଣ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକ୍ରମ

family_criteria.family_definition: Universal access — free eye care, cataract surgery, and visual-impairment services are available to every person in India regardless of income, age, or family size. School children are specifically covered for refractive-error screening and free spectacles.

covered_procedures: Free cataract surgery (including intraocular lens / IOL) at government hospitals and empanelled NGO eye hospitals
Free eye check-ups and screening at outreach eye camps in rural areas
Free treatment and management of other eye diseases (glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, corneal blindness)
Free distribution of spectacles to school children with refractive errors
Free spectacles for near vision to the elderly (presbyopia) where provided
Corneal transplantation and eye banking services (collection and distribution of donated corneas)
Treatment of childhood blindness and squint correction
Diabetic retinopathy screening and laser treatment at designated centres
Vision centres and eye care services integrated with primary health care
excluded_procedures: Private hospital cataract or eye surgery charges
Premium / cosmetic intraocular lenses beyond the standard IOL provided
Refractive (LASIK) surgery for cosmetic vision correction

required_documents.name: Any Government Photo ID
required_documents.notes: Helpful for hospital registration and follow-up, but free eye screening and cataract surgery at camps and government hospitals are not denied for lack of ID.

enrollment_channels: Government district hospital eye / ophthalmology department
Empanelled NGO and private eye hospitals (under NPCBVI tie-up)
Free eye screening camps in villages and urban areas
Vision Centre at Primary Health Centre level
ASHA / ANM community health worker referral
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Attend a free eye screening camp in your area, or visit the eye department of a government district hospital or a vision centre
Step 2: Get a free eye examination by an ophthalmologist or ophthalmic assistant
Step 3: If a cataract is detected, you are registered for free cataract surgery at a government or empanelled NGO eye hospital
Step 4: Undergo free cataract surgery with an intraocular lens (IOL) implant
Step 5: Receive post-operative care, medicines, and follow-up free of cost
Step 6: For refractive errors, school children and others receive free spectacles
Step 7: Other eye conditions (glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, corneal blindness) are treated or referred to specialized centres at no cost
description_for_embedding: The National Programme for Control of Blindness and Visual Impairment (NPCBVI), originally launched in 1976 as the National Programme for Control of Blindness and renamed in 2017, is a central government programme implemented within the National Health Mission framework to reduce avoidable blindness in India. It provides FREE cataract surgery in government hospitals, district health centres, and empanelled NGO-run eye hospitals, and funds outreach eye camps where free eye check-ups and cataract operations are conducted in rural areas — all with no income test or means assessment. Cataract is the leading cause of blindness in India, and free cataract surgery with intraocular lens (IOL) implantation is the programme's flagship service; the Mission Mode Cataract Surgery campaign (Netra Jyoti Abhiyan) has cleared large backlogs of eligible cases. Beyond cataract, NPCBVI covers treatment of other eye diseases such as glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and corneal blindness, supports eye banking and corneal transplantation, screens school children for refractive errors and distributes free spectacles, and manages childhood blindness. Primary and secondary eye care is delivered through district hospitals, regional institutes of ophthalmology, vision centres at primary health level, and District Blindness Control Societies. Anyone with poor vision, suspected cataract, or any eye problem — especially the elderly — can get a free eye check-up at a government eye camp or district hospital and receive free cataract surgery if needed. The programme is centrally funded with cost-sharing arrangements with States under NHM.
